data.description:
## Why
The epic's headline deliverable is a STABLE internal API both review gates consume — one implementation, not
two. This story integrates Engine A, T0, and Engine B behind the three query surfaces and pins the
consumer-facing contract so `5fd2` (DET floor P2/P3/P4) and `9da1` (Pass-2 grounding) can build against it.

## What
- The public **oracle API** exposing the three query surfaces over the engines: `refute_absence(reference)`,
  `applies(dimension)`, `scan(...)` — each returning the normalized three-valued evidence model + coverage.
- The **consumer integration contract**: the reference-in schema (what review prompts must emit), the dimension/
  detector identifiers, and how coverage is surfaced — documented so consumers wire up deterministically.
- **Docs**: how the oracle works, the fail-open behavior, the detector envelope format, the `.rebar/detectors/`
  convention, and the CLI/library/MCP entry points (read-only; mirrors existing rebar read-tool patterns).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py` (public surface) + a thin CLI/MCP read-tool exposure + `docs/`.
- Depends on S2 (Engine A), S3 (T0), S4 (Engine B). Pure-evidence: no block/advisory logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stable public API for the three query surfaces over the three engines, returning the evidence model + coverage.
- [ ] Consumer integration contract documented (reference schema, dimension IDs, coverage surfacing); `5fd2`/`9da1` can build against it.
- [ ] Read-only CLI/library (and MCP read-tool) entry points consistent with existing rebar read tools.
- [ ] Docs: API, fail-open semantics, detector format, `.rebar/detectors/` convention.
data.title: Public oracle API (3 query surfaces) + consumer integration contract + docs
